creator: brooks@FreeBSD.org
text: In CheriBSD's cheri-purecap-kernel branch (https://github.com/CTSRD-CHERI/cheribsd/tree/cheri-purecap-kernel) we have a modification that reduces this to a single pointer to a structure holding the other pointers.  In our case this is because the in-kernel use includes too many pointers to fit when you blow them up to 128-bit.  This doesn't address the leakage issues, but might be a place to start from.
